title: bank of canada / consumer expectations / inflation expectations | Data Release Other | Canadian Survey of Consumer Expectations—Fourth Quarter of 2...
summary: The Bank of Canada released fourth-quarter 2025 results from its Canadian Survey of Consumer Expectations, showing that concerns over high prices and economic uncertainty related to trade conflict continue to weigh on consumer sentiment. Despite a slight improvement in labour market conditions, the CSCE indicator declined slightly. Near-term inflation expectations remain elevated relative to pre-pandemic levels, while long-term inflation expectations have eased below pre-pandemic benchmarks.
